100.$description: For slightly raised elevation to give a subtle emphasis to elements on the page background. Examples: alerts and avatars

overflow.$description: For elements that remain fixed, staying in view on scroll, to appear above scrolling content. Examples: back to top and summary action bar

overlay.$description: Overlay elevation is used for elements that are temporal and more visually distinct via higher elevation from the surface.

100.$description: For a small overlay elevation on temporal elements that are easily dismissed. Examples: popovers, dropdown menus, and picker inputs

200.$description: For an overlay elevation that gives more emphasis to larger elements that are used in conjunction with the main page. Example: flyouts

300.$description: For floating overlays that require clear visual separation. Use for interruptive elements. Examples: modals and toasts
